seoTitle: West Ipswich vs West Mackay — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between West Ipswich and West Mackay. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: West Ipswich vs West Mackay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in West Ipswich and West Mackay.
heading: West Ipswich vs West Mackay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between West Ipswich, Queensland and West Mackay, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: West Mackay has a lower overall climate risk score (25/100) compared to West Ipswich (47/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is West Ipswich or West Mackay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: West Ipswich has a flood risk score of 53/100, while West Mackay scores 28/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: West Ipswich scores 58/100 for bushfire risk. West Mackay scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in West Ipswich or West Mackay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. West Mackay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.57% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
